arts.ttl: What we learnt from Hull City's first 10 Championship games as mistakes leave no hiding place
arts.sum: The ten-game point of any season is where progress is often assessed, and Hull City reached that on Sunday afternoon when they were narrowly beaten 1-0 by Sunderland for the second successive campaign. City’s three wins all came against teams in poor form, and the four defeats have come - without scoring a goal - against teams higher than them in the table, so perhaps that gives an indication of how the season may play out in what looks increasingly like one of transition.

arts.ttl: Hull City v Burnley: Team news, predicted teams and TV details as Clarets bid to extend good run
arts.sum: Burnley will be out to make it back-to-back wins when they make the midweek trip to Humberside tonight to take on Hull City.

arts.ttl: Potential Hull City XI for Burnley with Millar and Zambrano set to return
arts.sum: The Tigers boss made four changes to his starting XI for Sunday’s 1-0 home defeat to Sunderland, handing a home debut to Charlie Hughes and a debut to Bayer Leverkusen starlet Gustavo Puerta, while there were returns for Xavier Simons and Abu Kamara. And the German head coach may well look to bring back Millar into the starting XI and be tempted to hand a full debut to Joao Pedro, who came off the bench in the closing stages at the weekend.

arts.sum: Scott Parker has hinted he may look to make changes to freshen up his Burnley side for tonight’s clash against Hull City.

arts.ttl: Charlie Hughes opens up on Hull City transfer after making big Wigan Athletic decision
arts.sum: Charlie Hughes says his decision to move to Hull City was the perfect step in his fledgling career as he looks to settle into life as a Championship footballer. "I just cracked on every single day, and I never really looked at my phone; I just let it happen, and then the first bit of interest I heard, I still continued to play and train and then do my homework, having a look at Hull playing over their pre-season games.
